rules.name: Ensure the S3 bucket has access logging enabled

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket does not have access logging enabled.

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket access logs provide a record of all requests made to the bucket. Without logging, unauthorized or anomalous access cannot be detected or investigated.

help.text: Add a logging block to your aws_s3_bucket resource: logging { target_bucket = aws_s3_bucket.log_bucket.id }

rules.name: Ensure S3 bucket has block public ACLS enabled

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket does not block public ACLs.

fullDescription.text: S3 buckets should block public ACLs to prevent accidental or intentional exposure of sensitive objects. Without this setting, any object can be made publicly accessible via an ACL.

help.text: Add aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block with block_public_acls = true to your Terraform configuration.

rules.name: Ensure IAM policy does not allow write access without constraints

shortDescription.text: IAM policy grants broad write access.

fullDescription.text: IAM policies should follow the principle of least privilege. Policies with '*' actions or overly broad resource ARNs grant more access than needed.

help.text: Scope the IAM policy to specific actions and resource ARNs. Avoid using '*' as the Resource value.

message.text: S3 bucket 'pursh-phi-documents' does not have access logging enabled.
S3 bucket 'pursh-phi-documents' does not block public ACLs.
IAM policy 'pursh-backend-policy' allows s3:* on resource '*'.
